title: The cash-in-the-sofa saga that just won't go away for South Africa's president
content: A committee of MPs has been formed to look at the case against Cyril Ramaphosa, but he may survive a vote.

originalTitle: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
title: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
content: Only two survived after trekking more than 50km (30 miles) across the desert to alert the authorities.

originalTitle: Fall in official Ebola numbers appears to be good news but it's not that simple
title: Hooray! Fewer Sick Friends in Congo! Keep Up the Good Work, Africa!
content:
Hello, my awesome African friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, ready to share some super important news with you all. We live on such a big and beautiful continent, full of brave people and amazing animals! Sometimes, grown-ups talk about big problems, and that can make us feel a little bit worried. But guess what? Today, I have some news that will make us smile and feel hopeful! It's about a place called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. It's a wonderful country in our big African family.

What is Ebola?

First, let's talk about something called Ebola. It's a kind of sickness, like a very naughty tiny germ that sometimes makes people feel unwell. It's not like when you get a little cold or sniffles. Ebola can make people feel very, very tired, have a fever, and their tummy might hurt. It's important to remember that Ebola is rare, and doctors and nurses work super hard to help people who get sick. They are like real-life superheroes! Did you know? The sickness called Ebola is named after a river in the Democratic Republic of Congo! Rivers are usually beautiful and give us water, but this one accidentally gave its name to a germ many years ago.

Good News from Congo!

Now for the happy news! A little while ago, doctors and health workers thought that a lot of people might have gotten sick with Ebola in DR Congo. They were counting how many people might have the tiny germ. But guess what? They have checked again, very carefully! And the new count is much, much smaller! They first thought there could be many more, but now they are sure about 380 people being sick with Ebola. That's a smaller number than they first guessed. What does "confirmed cases" mean? It means doctors have done special tests to be super sure that these people have the Ebola germ. It's like finding out exactly how many apples are in a basket, not just guessing from afar. This is good news because it means fewer people are confirmed to have the sickness right now compared to what everyone first thought. It means the efforts to stop the germ are working!

Why is this Good News?

This news makes us feel happy because it shows that the hard work of so many people is really helping! It means doctors, nurses, and community helpers in DR Congo are doing an amazing job. They are helping people get better and teaching everyone how to stay safe from the tiny germ. This makes us all hopeful for a very healthy future for our friends in DR Congo. When we hear numbers go down, it's like when you're playing a game and your team starts to win! It gives everyone more energy and smiles to keep going. Did you know? Doctors and nurses often wear special protective suits that look like astronaut costumes! These suits help keep them safe from germs while they take care of sick people. They are very brave!

But We Still Need to Be Careful!

Even though the numbers are smaller, it's important to remember that the Ebola germ is still around. It's like when you're cleaning your room – you might have put away most of your toys, but you still need to check under the bed for one or two more! So, everyone in DR Congo and other places needs to keep doing the smart things that help stop the sickness from spreading. We all need to keep learning and listening to our health heroes. What are some smart things we can do? One of the very best things is to wash your hands with soap and water! This washes away all the naughty germs that might be hiding on your hands. We also need to remember not to touch people who are sick without special protection, and to always go to a doctor if we feel very unwell. Our bodies are very important, and we must take good care of them! Did you know? Washing your hands with soap and water for 20 seconds is how long it takes to sing the "Happy Birthday" song two times! Try it next time you wash your hands!

Our African Heroes!

Africa is full of heroes! Not just the doctors and nurses, but also the brave community leaders and even families who are helping each other. They teach their neighbours about staying safe, and they share important information. Many smart scientists from Africa and all over the world are also working super hard. They are trying to find special medicines and vaccines. A vaccine is like a special shield you get for your body to help you fight off germs before you even get sick! When everyone works together, we can make big problems seem smaller. This is what we call "Ubuntu" in some parts of Africa – it means "I am because we are." We are stronger together! Did you know? Many African scientists are leading the way in finding new ways to keep our communities healthy! They are using their brilliant minds to help everyone.

Staying Safe and Strong!

So, let's celebrate this good news that fewer people are sick with Ebola in DR Congo! It shows that hope is strong, and hard work pays off. But let's also remember to keep being careful and smart. Always remember to wash your hands, listen to your parents and teachers about staying healthy, and be kind to everyone. Together, we can help keep all our communities across Africa strong and happy! Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! Let's keep cheering for our heroes and working for a healthier Africa!
summary: Good news from DR Congo! Fewer people are confirmed to have Ebola than first thought, showing that hard work by doctors and communities is helping, but we still need to be careful and keep working together to stay safe.
whyMatters: This news matters because it tells us that fewer friends are sick with a serious illness, which makes us happy and hopeful. It also shows that when people work together, like doctors, nurses, and families, they can make a big difference and help keep everyone in our African family safe and healthy.

originalTitle: US doctor recovers from Ebola in Germany as DRC cases surge to 488
title: US doctor recovers from Ebola in Germany as DRC cases surge to 488
content: Berlin's Charite hospital hailed treatment as a 'significant therapeutic success' as outbreak spreads in DR Congo.

title: Hegseth attacks Europe over 'invasion' of migrants on its beaches in D-Day speech
content: The US defence secretary was speaking in Normandy, 82 years after allied forces launched their operation to liberate Nazi-occupied north-western Europe.

originalTitle: Sullivan steps down at West Ham in wake of historical allegations
title: Sullivan steps down at West Ham in wake of historical allegations
content: West Ham United co-owner David Sullivan steps down as joint chairman of the club with immediate effect following a joint investigation by BBC Panorama and the Times newspaper into his behaviour.

originalTitle: Gemmill beats McTominay in vote for your most iconic Scotland goal
title: Yay! Archie Gemmill's Super Goal is Scotland's Best Ever!
content:
Jambo, my wonderful little friends! Your friend, the news reporter, is here with some super exciting news from the world of football! Do you love watching people kick a ball around? I know I do! Today, we're going to talk about a very special football moment that people in a country called Scotland have chosen as their very best!

What is Football?

Football, or soccer as some call it, is a game played with a round ball. Two teams try to kick the ball into the other team's net. When the ball goes into the net, it’s called a "goal"! Goals are the most exciting part of the game! Everyone cheers loudly when a team scores a goal. It's like a big party every time the ball hits the net! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the whole world! So many people play it and watch it.

Goals, Goals, Goals!

Imagine someone running really fast with the ball, dribbling it past many other players, and then *WHOOSH!* scoring a goal! Some goals are just normal, but some are super-duper special. They are so clever and amazing that people talk about them for a very long time. It’s like when you draw a picture that is so beautiful, everyone wants to see it again and again. Special goals are like that! They make us gasp and cheer!

A Big Competition to Choose the Best!

Guess what? People in Scotland had a big competition! No, it wasn't a running race or a singing contest. It was a competition to choose their *most iconic* goal. "Iconic" means very, very famous and remembered for many, many years. It’s a goal that makes everyone happy and proud when they think about it. People had to vote, like putting a tick next to their favourite flavour of ice cream. Lots and lots of people voted for their number one goal! Did you know? Voting means choosing your favourite thing from a list. Grown-ups vote for leaders, and sometimes we vote for our favourite snacks too!

Meet Archie Gemmill!

And the winner is... Archie Gemmill! He is a very famous football player from a long time ago. Imagine your grandpa or grandma, he played football even before they were born! But his special goal is still talked about today, like it happened yesterday! He was a very skillful player, which means he was very good at kicking and controlling the ball with his feet. He made football look easy and fun!

The Amazing Goal!

Now, let me tell you about Archie Gemmill’s amazing goal! It happened in a very, very big football game called the World Cup. This was in the year 1978. That's a super long time ago! Scotland was playing against a team called the Netherlands. The Netherlands team was very strong, with many excellent players. Archie got the ball, and he started to run. He ran past one player, then another player, then another one! He was like a speedy little ant running away from big elephants! He kept the ball so close to his feet, it looked like magic! He dribbled past four different players. Can you imagine that? Four players tried to stop him, but he was too fast and too clever! It was a brilliant, brave run! Then, when he was very close to the goal, he kicked the ball with his foot, and *BAM!* The ball flew into the net! The crowd went wild! Everyone was shouting and cheering with happy voices! It was such a beautiful goal because he did it all by himself, running past so many people. It was a solo effort, which means he did it all alone with his super skills! What a fantastic moment!

What is the World Cup?

The World Cup is the biggest football party in the whole wide world! Teams from different countries, like Kenya, South Africa, Brazil, and Scotland, come together to play against each other. It happens every four years. It’s like a super big championship to find out which country has the best football team on the planet. Everyone watches on TV, and it’s very exciting! It brings people together! Did you know? The World Cup trophy is a very shiny gold cup. Only the winning team gets to lift it high and show it to the whole world!

Why is This Goal So Special?

Archie Gemmill's goal is special because it was so brave and so skillful. He wasn't scared to take on many big, strong players. It was a moment of pure magic that made everyone in Scotland feel very proud and happy. Even though Scotland didn't win that World Cup, this goal is remembered as a moment of great joy and incredible skill. It shows that even in tough games, you can do something absolutely amazing! When people talk about Scotland's football history, they always talk about Archie Gemmill's goal. It's like a super happy memory for everyone. It makes people smile and remember how good their team could be. It gives them hope and pride for their country's football. It’s a story told from generation to generation!

Other Amazing Goals

Of course, there are many other wonderful goals in football. Another player named Scott McTominay also scored a fantastic goal recently. Many people loved his goal too! He is a very good player for Scotland right now. But when everyone voted, Archie Gemmill's goal from a very long time ago was chosen as the most special, the "most iconic" one. It shows how some moments just stay in our hearts and minds forever! Did you know? Football players practice every day, sometimes for many hours, to become super good at scoring goals and playing as a team!

The Joy of Football!

So, there you have it, my little friends! A super story about a super goal chosen by super fans in Scotland! It reminds us that football is full of excitement, incredible skill, and wonderful memories that last a lifetime. Whether it's a game in your local park or a big World Cup match, every goal is a reason to cheer and celebrate! Keep playing, keep watching, and keep cheering for your favourite teams! See you next time with more exciting news from around Africa and the world! Kwaharini!
summary: People in Scotland voted, and they picked Archie Gemmill's amazing solo goal from the 1978 World Cup as their most famous and best goal ever!
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how one amazing moment in football can make people proud and happy for a very long time, even many years later. It's a reminder of great skill and joy.
